title: Are Expressive Encoders Necessary for Discrete Graph Generation?
summary: arXiv:2603.08825v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Discrete graph generation has emerged as a powerful paradigm for modeling graph data, often relying on highly expressive neural backbones such as transformers or higher-order architectures. We revisit this design choice by introducing GenGNN, a modular message-passing framework for graph generation.

title: OPENXRD: A Comprehensive Benchmark Framework for LLM/MLLM XRD Question Answering
summary: arXiv:2507.09155v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: We introduce OPENXRD, a comprehensive benchmarking framework for evaluating large language models (LLMs) and multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) in crystallography question answering. title: MASEval: Extending Multi-Agent Evaluation from Models to Systems
summary: arXiv:2603.08835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid adoption of LLM-based agentic systems has produced a rich ecosystem of frameworks (smolagents, LangGraph, AutoGen, CAMEL, LlamaIndex, i.a.). title: DataFactory: Collaborative Multi-Agent Framework for Advanced Table Question Answering
summary: arXiv:2603.09152v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Table Question Answering (TableQA) enables natural language interaction with structured tabular data. title: Real-Time Trust Verification for Safe Agentic Actions using TrustBench
summary: arXiv:2603.09157v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As large language models evolve from conversational assistants to autonomous agents, ensuring trustworthiness requires a fundamental shift from post-hoc evaluation to real-time action verification. title: GenePlan: Evolving Better Generalized PDDL Plans using Large Language Models
summary: arXiv:2603.09481v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present GenePlan (GENeralized Evolutionary Planner), a novel framework that leverages large language model (LLM) assisted evolutionary algorithms to generate domain-dependent generalized planners for classical planning tasks described in PDDL. title: OOD-MMSafe: Advancing MLLM Safety from Harmful Intent to Hidden Consequences
summary: arXiv:2603.09706v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While safety alignment for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) has gained significant attention, current paradigms primarily target malicious intent or situational violations. We propose shifting the safety frontier toward consequence-driven safety, a paradigm essential for the robust deployment

title: Does the Question Really Matter? Training-Free Data Selection for Vision-Language SFT
summary: arXiv:2603.09715v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Visual instruction tuning is crucial for improving vision-language large models (VLLMs). However, many samples can be solved via linguistic patterns or common-sense shortcuts, without genuine cross-modal reasoning, limiting the effectiveness of multimodal learning. title: Arbiter: Detecting Interference in LLM Agent System Prompts
summary: arXiv:2603.08993v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: System prompts for LLM-based coding agents are software artifacts that govern agent behavior, yet lack the testing infrastructure applied to conventional software. title: Reviving ConvNeXt for Efficient Convolutional Diffusion Models
summary: arXiv:2603.09408v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Recent diffusion models increasingly favor Transformer backbones, motivated by the remarkable scalability of fully attentional architectures. title: Variational Routing: A Scalable Bayesian Framework for Calibrated Mixture-of-Experts Transformers
summary: arXiv:2603.09453v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Foundation models are increasingly being deployed in contexts where understanding the uncertainty of their outputs is critical to ensuring responsible deployment. title: EXPLORE-Bench: Egocentric Scene Prediction with Long-Horizon Reasoning
summary: arXiv:2603.09731v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly considered as a foundation for embodied agents, yet it remains unclear whether they can reliably reason about the long-term physical consequences of actions from an egocentric viewpoint. title: Correction of Transformer-Based Models with Smoothing Pseudo-Projector
summary: arXiv:2603.09815v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: The pseudo-projector is a lightweight modification that can be integrated into existing language models and other neural networks without altering their core architecture. title: Daily-Omni: Towards Audio-Visual Reasoning with Temporal Alignment across Modalities
summary: arXiv:2505.17862v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Recent Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve promising performance on visual and audio benchmarks independently. title: MMGraphRAG: Bridging Vision and Language with Interpretable Multimodal Knowledge Graphs
summary: arXiv:2507.20804v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) often suffer from hallucinations, which Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and GraphRAG mitigate by incorporating external knowledge and knowledge graphs (KGs).
